description: Bootstrap ClawMem, inject recall context, mirror turns, and close conversation issues on session end.

hooks.prompt:
You are finalizing a ClawMem conversation. Read the transcript and store durable memory.

Hook input (JSON):
$ARGUMENTS

Steps:
1. Parse the JSON above. Extract `transcript_path` and `session_id`. If `transcript_path` is missing or the file is empty/unreadable, skip to step 7 with `candidates_stored = 0` and reason `finalize skipped: transcript unavailable`.
2. Use the Read tool on `transcript_path`. The file is JSONL: one JSON object per line. Keep only entries where `type` is `"user"` or `"assistant"` and `message.content` contains plain text. Ignore tool_use and tool_result entries. Extract the text from `message.content` (it is either a string or an array of parts like `[{"type":"text","text":"..."}]`).
3. Call `mcp__clawmem__memory_labels` once to fetch the current reusable schema (kinds and topics). Prefer these existing labels when they fit; only invent a new short machine-readable label when nothing fits.
4. Internally produce a JSON plan (do not emit it) of the form `{summary, title, candidates:[{title, detail, kind, topics}]}`.
   - summary: 2-4 factual sentences, no markdown, no bullets.
   - title: under 50 chars, same language as the majority of the transcript, specific.
   - candidates: one durable fact per candidate. Extract only: stable preferences, decisions, constraints, workflows, lessons, ongoing tasks. Skip tool chatter, session boilerplate, transient requests. Each candidate needs `title` (concise) and `detail` (full durable fact). `kind` must be from the fetched schema if any fits; otherwise one short stable machine-readable new label. `topics` optional; reuse schema topics first. Write candidate title and detail in the conversation's majority language. If nothing durable was learned, `candidates = []`.
5. For each candidate, call `mcp__clawmem__memory_store` with `{title, detail, kind, topics}`. Count successful stores as `candidates_stored`. If `memory_store` reports the memory already exists (dedup), still count it as successful — the backend deduplicates by sha256(detail).
6. Append one event line to the plugin event log using the Bash tool. Run a single command that (a) ensures the debug directory exists, (b) computes an ISO 8601 timestamp, and (c) appends one JSON line via `jq -n` so every dynamic value is escaped automatically. It is YOUR responsibility to substitute two values: `<session_id>` from the hook input JSON above, and `<N>` the integer count of successfully stored candidates.

    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/debug" && \
    TS="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" && \
    jq -n \
      --arg ts "$TS" \
      --arg sid "<session_id from $ARGUMENTS>" \
      --argjson n <N, the number of successfully stored candidates as an integer> \
      '{ts:$ts, source:"hook", hook:"SessionEnd", type:"finalize_success", sessionId:$sid, candidates_stored:$n}' \
      >> "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/debug/events.jsonl"

   For the failure path (see step 7), use the same pattern but replace `"finalize_success"` with `"finalize_failed"` and add `--arg reason "<short error>"` plus a `reason:$reason` field in the jq object.
7. Return the agent-hook response as a single JSON object on the final line of your output, nothing else:
   `{"ok": true, "reason": "finalize complete: <N> stored"}` on success.
   `{"ok": true, "reason": "finalize skipped: <why>"}` when transcript is empty or unreadable (ok:true because there is nothing to save — not a failure).
   `{"ok": false, "reason": "<short error>"}` on any tool error. On failure also append a `finalize_failed` event using the jq pattern in step 6. Do not stop before writing the event line on failure.

Scope restriction: You must only use these tools — Read (on `transcript_path` or other files Claude Code explicitly references in the hook input), Bash (only for the `mkdir`, `date`, and `jq -n` commands shown above), and the MCP tools `mcp__clawmem__memory_labels` and `mcp__clawmem__memory_store`. Do not explore the repository, do not read files outside the ones the above steps explicitly require, do not modify code, do not run tests, do not open unrelated URLs. This entire task must fit in roughly 5–10 tool calls.

You are finalizing a ClawMem conversation before context compaction. Read the transcript and store durable memory that would otherwise be lost when context is summarized.

Hook input (JSON):
$ARGUMENTS

Steps:
1. Parse the JSON above. Extract `transcript_path` and `session_id`. If `transcript_path` is missing or the file is empty/unreadable, skip to step 7 with `candidates_stored = 0` and reason `finalize skipped: transcript unavailable`.
2. Use the Read tool on `transcript_path`. The file is JSONL: one JSON object per line. Keep only entries where `type` is `"user"` or `"assistant"` and `message.content` contains plain text. Ignore tool_use and tool_result entries. Extract the text from `message.content` (string or `[{"type":"text","text":"..."}]`).
3. Call `mcp__clawmem__memory_labels` once to fetch the current reusable schema. Prefer these existing labels when they fit; only invent a new short machine-readable label when nothing fits.
4. Internally produce a JSON plan (do not emit it) of the form `{summary, title, candidates:[{title, detail, kind, topics}]}`.
   - candidates: one durable fact per candidate. Extract only: stable preferences, decisions, constraints, workflows, lessons, ongoing tasks that emerged since the start of the conversation (or since the previous compact, if you can tell). Skip tool chatter and transient requests. Each candidate needs `title` and `detail`; `kind` must be from the fetched schema if any fits, otherwise one short new label; `topics` optional. Write in the conversation's majority language. If nothing durable was learned, `candidates = []`.
5. For each candidate, call `mcp__clawmem__memory_store` with `{title, detail, kind, topics}`. Count successful stores as `candidates_stored`. Duplicate-detected responses still count as successful.
6. Append one event line to the plugin event log using the Bash tool. Run a single command that (a) ensures the debug directory exists, (b) computes an ISO 8601 timestamp, and (c) appends one JSON line via `jq -n` so every dynamic value is escaped automatically. It is YOUR responsibility to substitute two values: `<session_id>` from the hook input JSON above, and `<N>` the integer count of successfully stored candidates.

    mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/debug" && \
    TS="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" && \
    jq -n \
      --arg ts "$TS" \
      --arg sid "<session_id from $ARGUMENTS>" \
      --argjson n <N, the number of successfully stored candidates as an integer> \
      '{ts:$ts, source:"hook", hook:"PreCompact", type:"finalize_success", sessionId:$sid, candidates_stored:$n}' \
      >> "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA/debug/events.jsonl"

   For the failure path (see step 7), use the same pattern but replace `"finalize_success"` with `"finalize_failed"` and add `--arg reason "<short error>"` plus a `reason:$reason` field in the jq object.
7. Return the agent-hook response as a single JSON object on the final line, nothing else:
   `{"ok": true, "reason": "finalize complete: <N> stored"}` on success.
   `{"ok": true, "reason": "finalize skipped: <why>"}` when nothing to save.
   `{"ok": false, "reason": "<short error>"}` on tool error. On failure also append a `finalize_failed` event using the jq pattern in step 6.

Scope restriction: You must only use these tools — Read (on `transcript_path` or other files Claude Code explicitly references in the hook input), Bash (only for the `mkdir`, `date`, and `jq -n` commands shown above), and the MCP tools `mcp__clawmem__memory_labels` and `mcp__clawmem__memory_store`. Do not explore the repository, do not read files outside the ones the above steps explicitly require, do not modify code, do not run tests, do not open unrelated URLs. This entire task must fit in roughly 5–10 tool calls.
